calendar.matters:
Petition For Order Bringing Administration Of Trusts Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Oct-23-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Emilie Calhoun for trustee Yelena Ostrovsky, who was also present.
G. Franchesca Callejo for settlor Joan C. Benjamin.
Robyn Christo, Guardian Ad Litem for Joan Benjamin.
Kim and Daivd Benjamin, children of Joan Benjamin, were also present.

The matter was called for hearing on Petition for Order Bringing Administration of Trusts Under Court Supervision.

Discussion was held regarding changes in circumstances since the filing of the petition. The conservatorship had been terminated and the prior issues prompting court involvement had abated. Ms. Calhoun stated that Ms. Benjamin's children wished to move forward without court supervision.

The Court stated it had been inclined to grant supervision for a limited period but, in light of the current posture, intended to deny the petition. The matter was taken under submission with the intent to deny.

The Court authorized the trustee to pay from the trust reasonable attorney's fees, costs, and other expenses associated with the trust and the related conservatorship proceedings. The Court also authorized Ms. Callejo and Ms. Christo to file ex parte petitions for discharge from their roles.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Order Bringing Administration Of Trusts Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Jul-30-2025 Continued To Oct-22-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-24-307355 Conservatorship of Joan Benjamin and PTR--24-307356 In the Matter of Joan C. Benjamin 1987 Revocable Trust.

Appearances:
Emilie M. Calhoun appeared in person for and with Yelena Ostrovsky, Petitioner. (via zoom).
G. Franchesca Callejo, Court Appointed Attorney for Settlor in related conservatorship (via Zoom).
Robyn Christo, Court Appointed Guardian Ad Litem for Joan Benjamin. (via zoom)
Kelly Dixon for and with Daniel Cunningham, Conservator. (in person)
David Benjamin appeared in person.
Kim Benjamin appeared via zoom.

The Court waived the appearance of Ms. Benjamin from today's proceedings and also for the next appearance.

Discussion was held regarding the placement of Ms. Benjamin at the Belmont Village facility, that the Belmont Facility would be the best route for her, that she should be in a secure perimeter, and that she should be placed in memory care unit.

The Court ordered Ms. Dixon to prepare an order after hearing and circulate amongst her peers.

All matters are continued to October 22, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Status on placement is set for October 22, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition For Order Bringing Administration Of Trusts Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Jun-25-2025 Continued To Jul-30-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Filing Petition To Bring Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar Jun-23-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re: Filing Petition To Bring Trust Under Court Supervision
Off Calendar May-05-2025 Continued To Jun-23-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Instructions Re: 1. Construction Of Trust Instrument(Including Invalidation Of Recent A
Off Calendar Mar-11-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Emilie M. Calhoun for Yelena Ostrovsky, Petitioner.
G. Franchesca Callejo, Court Appointed Attorney for Settlor in related conservatorship (via Zoom).
Robyn Christo, Court Appointed Guardian Ad Litem for Joan Benjamin.
Kelly Dixon for Daniel Cunningham, Conservator.

The Court expressed concern about the trust moving forward without court supervision, questioning whether that approach was appropriate in this case. Ms. Callejo stated that due to the difficult nature of the case, she believed that it should be under court supervision. Ms. Callejo agreed to file a petition to bring trust under court supervision. A status hearing was set for May 5, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, regarding the filing of the petition to bring the trust under court supervision. The court took the petition for instructions under submission with the intent to grant.

The Court inquired whether all parties had reviewed Ms. Christo's report regarding the trust. No objections were raised to the reasonableness of the fees requested. The Court approved the invoice and confirmed that no further petition would be required for fee approval and stated it would issue an order after hearing.

The Court was informed that Joan Benjamin is currently in San Diego with her son and that there are ongoing disputes regarding her future living arrangements. Ms. Christo raised concerns about the conservatorship proceedings and requested clarification on whether she should continue representing Ms. Benjamin. The Court expanded Ms. Christo's appointment to include the conservatorship matter, directing her to investigate Ms. Benjamin's health, well-being, and placement status. Ms. Christo is to file a status report (in conservatorship matter PCN-24-307355) by April 1, 2025, regarding Ms. Benjamin's living situation. The Court set a status hearing in the conservatorship (PCN-24-307355) for April 7, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204, regarding Ms. Benjamin's placement.

Judge: Ross C. Moody; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

Petition For Instructions Re: 1. Construction Of Trust Instrument(Including Invalidation Of Recent A
Off Calendar Jan-27-2025 Continued To Mar-10-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (in person):

Emille Calhoun, Esq., for and with Yelena Ostrovsky, Petitioner.
Franchesca Callejo, Esq., for Joan Benjamin, Conservatee (Conservatee's appearance is waived at Counsel's request).
Kelly Dixon, Esq., for and with Daniel Cunningham, Conservator.
Robyn Christo, Esq., court-appointed Guardian ad Litem, for Conservatee.

At the outset, the court addresses the Opposition filed on Dec-09-2024. The court notes that it intends to strike the filing. No objections to the court's intention are presented. The court strikes the "PROBATE OPPOSITION/OBJECTION TO PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS RE: 1. CONSTRUCTION OF TRUST INSTRUMENT (INCLUDING INVALIDATION OF RECENT AMENDMENT); AND 2. AFFIRMING YELENA OSTROVSKY'S STATUS AS TRUSTEE " filed on Dec-09-2024 by Objector Pamela D. Wilson, in open court.

At the court's query, Ms. Christo offers status regarding reviewing the pleadings and meeting with the parties. Ms. Christo advises that she has a good sense regarding the proceedings in this matter but would like additional time to complete and reduce her findings to writing. The court then announces that it intends to place the subject Trust under court supervision. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the court's intent. Ms. Calhoun believes that placing the Trust under court supervision will cause additional costs and requests that the court consider waiting until Ms. Christo files her report to determine whether to place the Trust under court supervision. No objections to Ms. Calhoun's request are presented.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Mar-10-2025 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.

Discussion was held regarding a possible appointment for Guardian ad Litem for Ms. Benjamin, that an Evidentiary hearing should take place for regarding her capacity, and that there is a pending objection on file for this matter. The Court encourages MS. Callejo to file a drop off exparte for Guardian ad Litem. This matter is continued to January 27, 2025 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
